sourceMovie.title: Divergent
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Divergent (2014) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Neil Burger's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Divergent clicked for you, The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2 (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Oblivion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Divergent clicked for you, Oblivion (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Divergent?
faq.answer: Try The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2, Oblivion, Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets—each shares DNA with Divergent while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Divergent?
faq.answer: Divergent (2014) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Divergent part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Divergent Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
